One man dead in Port Coquitlam shooting   Homicide detectives have been called in to investigate.

Douglas Todd: Metro Vancouver has 650 homes worth $10 million+. That's 13 times higher than 2010   Analysis: The number of detached mansions valued at $10 million-plus in the Lower Mainland is now 13 times higher than in 2010. Global capital helps explain the extraordinary expansion of megamansions.

Officer was 'choked,' four people arrested in 'interaction' at the National War Memorial: Ottawa police   An initial investigation found an interaction between officers and individuals at the site became “confrontational” after one officer was choked.

Canada’s Supreme Court says expanded rape shield laws are constitutional   Such laws began appearing four decades ago to prevent a complainant in a sexual assault case from having evidence of their sexual history used to discredit them

Some Texas schools may call slavery 'involuntary relocation'   A group of nine educators submitted the idea to the State Board of Education as part of Texas’ efforts to develop new social studies curriculum

Tesla hit by new lawsuit alleging racial abuse against Black workers   The workers said they were subjected to offensive racist comments and behavior by colleagues, managers, and human resources employees on a regular basis.

Suspected truck driver in Texas migrant deaths was on meth: lawmaker   Fifty-three migrants lost their lives in sweltering heat, making it the deadliest such trafficking incident on record in the United States
TSX tumbles nearly 14 per cent in second quarter amid recession fears   Wall Street also ends lower Thursday as S&P 500 posts worst first half in more than half a century

Compensation questions loom for Air Canada customers with cancelled flights   Canada's largest carrier to cut more than 15 per cent of its flights in July and August as flight network sags under travel resurgence
U.S. Supreme Court decision may slow transition to cleaner energy   New ruling imposes constraints on what the Environmental Protection Agency can do without exceeding its legal authority
B.C. Ferries cancels sailings before long weekend because of mechanical issues   Sailings on the Queen of Alberni between Horseshoe Bay and Departure Bay have been cancelled Thursday.

Vancouver city council approves $3.49-billion, four-year capital plan   The new plan represents a roughly 26 per cent increase over the previous four-year plan, which staff said was largely to address the city's aging infrastructure.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.

Secrecy in the courts: Exclusive study reveals increasing use of publication bans in Canada   An investigation of discretionary publication bans requested during the past two years across four provinces shows a 25 per cent increase from one year to the next

Vaughn Palmer: B.C. NDP rivals might have to band together in anybody-but-Eby movement   Opinion: If David Eby does run for the NDP leadership, other candidates would be hard-pressed to claim as many accomplishments calculated to appeal to NDP voters.
Chris Selley: At long last, Hockey Canada's judgment day approaches   If major junior hockey doesn't see a moral imperative for reform, an economic one will have to do

John Ivison: Transport minister thinks he's done all he can to alleviate airport chaos. He's wrong   Opinion: Not only can Omar Alghabra not guarantee a safe and efficient system, he can’t even offer passengers faint hope about when things will get better

Vaughn Palmer: A rare premier makes a rare departure   The premier is leaving on his own terms, with a sustained approval rating that has made him one of the most popular premiers in the country through five years in office.

Vaughn Palmer: Hosting World Cup for the fun of hosting it, is the only part of John Horgan's rationale you can take to the bank   Opinion: “There’s going to be a return on that investment,” said Tourism Minister Melanie Mark. “We're looking at $1 billion coming to B.C."

Lions 34, Redblacks 31: B.C. passes the adversity test on the road to remain undefeated   Nathan Rourke has three turnovers, but steadies the ship late in the game to lead B.C. to victory over Ottawa.

Rolling Vancouver Whitecaps aim to topple MLS giants LAFC   Since the beginning of May, Vancouver has posted a solid 8-2-2 record across all competitions

Tim Hortons 'suspends support' for world juniors in another blow for Hockey Canada   The move comes after Scotiabank, Canadian Tire and Telus all paused Hockey Canada sponsorships Tuesday until the companies are confident the right steps are being taken to improve the sport’s culture.
Canucks Under the Microscope: Brock Boeser   After a very difficult 2021-22, can Vancouver Canucks winger Brock Boeser find new happiness on the ice in 2022-23?

Book review: Vancouver writer probes Holocaust’s wounds in searing, healing memoir   Journalist Marsha Lederman, the child of Holocaust survivors, takes on a daunting challenge in the brave, honest book Kiss the Red Stairs
Women in Film & TV Vancouver welcomes participants in emerging TV producer program   Six-month initiative aims to help women and gender-diverse creators break into the television industry

Vancouver's KiSS FM has been playing Rage Against the Machine on repeat. Why?   It became evident on Thursday morning that it was likely all a publicity stunt, as the company announced it was launching a new rock station on 104.9 Vancouver, 107.5 Chilliwack, and 92.5 Abbotsford called Sonic Radio.

10 great Canadian-shot TV shows you can stream this Canada Day long weekend   From The Boys to Handmaid's Tale to Reacher, let's celebrate our country by streaming some series that were filmed right here in Canada.
Former B.C. news anchor Deb Hope appointed to Order of Canada   Hope retired in 2014 after she was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease.
The It List: Marni and Uniqlo team up for stylish summer release   Two stylish brands have teamed up for a sweet summer style collaboration.
Recipe: A simply delicious steak   Summer barbecue season is upon us, which means it's the perfect time for a steak.

Health Canada backs down from nutrition warning labels for ground meat after criticism   New regulations will require that most foods containing more than 15 per cent of the recommended daily intake of sodium be labelled

Restaurant review: A nice slice of D.I.C.E.D., unprofitable by design   D.I.C.E.D. operates a restaurant, catering company, cooking school and meal delivery program but is primarily a social enterprise business

Condo Smarts: Successful strata communities result from reasonable behaviour, respect   When bylaws and rules are not enforced, troublesome individuals can take advantage.

Rare rock-star vintage photos pair with vintage and contemporary pieces in stunning Vancouver exhibition   A trio of giant photos of Led Zeppelin and the Mission Raceway are face-mounted onto coloured acrylic.
These spring-flowering bulbs are less likely to run amok   Fancier types like double-flowered Blue Spike and feathery Plumosum are not so prone to spread in a weedy fashion
Sold (Bought): Home offers lofty vistas from Vancouver House   This particular home forms part of the Estate Series — upper-level residences with luxuriously appointed interiors.
Hue development in Port Moody a showcase of progressive design   The development will consist of 222 homes, and is being billed as a design-forward project focusing on function and livability.
Montréal neighbourhood Saint-Henri a cultural delight   What’s old is new again in trendy and historic area.
Juhl: 3 road-trip games to keep the kids from driving you around the bend   These ideas will help you pass the time in the car and you don't have to buy anything to play.
Oregon- the land of outdoor adventures and indoor comforts    We stop often to enjoy sweeping views over the roaring surf or hike along the sandstone cliffs
B.C. lodges say it is a good time to "Go fish"   The B.C. fishing industry is expecting one of their best years in recent times

Buntzen Lake reserved parking now in effect: Here's how to book   As of June 27, anyone driving to Anmore's Buntzen Lake needs to reserve a parking spot. Here's what you need to know about the new system.

Apple eyes fuel purchases from dashboard as it revs up car software   A new feature will allow CarPlay users to tap an app to navigate to a pump and buy gas straight from a screen in the car
German court doubles red-light runner's fine due to SUV size   The penalty was set higher because the sport-ute allegedly posed more risk to pedestrians than a smaller car
Cross-Canada Road Trip: Whitehorse to Toronto in two 2022 Toyota Tundras   Some 5,500 km and seven days of driving through one magnificent country show the new-gen truck is just as stout as the old

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Machado: COVID has made socializing a little like having sex without protection   Getting back to some kind of normal means accepting the risk of COVID, and understanding that the feeling of liberation comes along with fear and uncertainty.
What are the signs of Parkinson's disease?   For people living with Parkinson's disease, exercise and eating a balanced diet may help control symptoms and even slow progression of the disease.
Ontario man accused of impaired driving after cannabis and alcohol found inside his vehicle   Man taken into custody after the OPP received multiple complaints about his driving on Highway 12
Over half of people using medical cannabis to combat chronic pain had withdrawal symptoms: study   Clusters of symptoms occur after significant periods of not using marijuana.
Florida man's idea to hide two cannabis cigars in his shoe was a bust   Man arrested for weed possession and driving with a suspended licence.

Toronto-based company partners with Wiz Khalifa on line of psychedelic and functional mushrooms   While the psychedelic mushrooms will only be available in the Netherlands, functional home-grow kits are set to launch in the U.S., Europe and Canada.

Lawyer looking to secure cannabis retail spot for client tried to bribe police chief   The lawyer approached a close relative of the police chief and offered that person $32,250 to speak to the chief.
